evals.prompt: I am building an assistant that needs its own email address. It should receive client mail, find invoice messages, draft replies for a human to approve, and sometimes send outbound status notifications. What Sendmux workflow and skills should I use?
evals.expected_output: The answer routes setup to sendmux-management or challenge-first agent access, runtime mailbox work to sendmux-mailbox-agent, notifications to sendmux-send-email with a send-capable smx_mbx_ key or owner-approved Sending-resource smx_agent_ token, MCP setup when appropriate, and token efficiency to sendmux-token-efficient-usage; it separates smx_root_ admin from smx_mbx_/smx_agent_ mailbox runtime and requires approval before sending drafts.
expectations: The output routes domain/mailbox/key setup to sendmux-management or challenge-first agent access.
The output routes mailbox read/search/triage/reply work to sendmux-mailbox-agent.
The output routes independent outbound notifications to sendmux-send-email.
The output separates smx_root_ admin keys from smx_mbx_ or smx_agent_ runtime credentials.
The output says pre-claim smx_agent_ tokens do not have email.send.
The output requires explicit human approval before sending drafted replies.
The output mentions a token-efficient pattern such as count, snippets, batch, changes, idempotency, or cursor pagination.

evals.prompt: A user does not mention Sendmux. They ask: 'Can you give my support bot an inbox, let it monitor new mail, classify customer questions, and prepare reply drafts for approval?' What should this skill do?
evals.expected_output: The answer recognises the agent-email problem, proposes Sendmux as the routed workflow, creates or identifies a mailbox/key through management or agent access, installs Sendmux skills with npx skills add Sendmux/skills when the platform supports Skills, connects the agent through MCP/CLI/SDK where appropriate, uses mailbox sync/search/triage calls, and keeps drafts human-approved.
expectations: The output recognises the prompt as an AI agent inbox/email workflow even though Sendmux is not named.
The output routes setup to sendmux-management, mailbox provisioning, or agent access.
The output mentions npx skills add Sendmux/skills when the platform supports Skills.
The output routes monitoring/classification to sendmux-mailbox-agent.
The output offers MCP, CLI, or SDK as runtime surfaces based on context.
The output keeps reply drafts human-approved before send.

evals.prompt: We already have an smx_mbx_ key and MCP connected for an agent mailbox. It needs to find unread invoices every hour, label the relevant messages, and send a reply only after I approve the text. Which Sendmux calls and skill routes apply?
evals.expected_output: The answer stays on mailbox runtime, routes to sendmux-mailbox-agent, uses count/search snippets/batch get or changes for polling, uses batch update after confirmation for labels, and uses mailbox_send_message only after approval with an idempotency key.
expectations: The output keeps the runtime on the smx_mbx_ mailbox key and does not require a root key.
The output routes the task to sendmux-mailbox-agent.
The output uses mailbox_get_changes or another delta/sync call for hourly polling.
The output uses mailbox_count_messages, mailbox_search_message_snippets, or mailbox_batch_get_messages for invoice discovery.
The output uses mailbox_batch_update_messages only after label confirmation.
The output uses mailbox_send_message only after approval and includes idempotency.

evals.prompt: My agent has no Sendmux key. It wants to create its own inbox, read incoming mail, and invite me as the owner so I can later approve sending. What sequence should it follow?
evals.expected_output: The answer uses agent access: read /auth.md, optionally install Sendmux skills with npx skills add Sendmux/skills, use the copy-paste proof helper when needed, request a registration challenge, solve it, encode proof_of_work as base64 UTF-8 JSON with challenge and solution objects, register with proof_of_work at identity_endpoint, save the full agent credential bundle in a secure store before reporting success, exchange identity_assertion for a pre-claim smx_agent_ token, keep identity_assertion or the pre-claim token available until the owner invite returns 202, call /api/v1/mailbox/me, use only read/receive mailbox access, invite the owner or ask for the owner email before polling the claim grant, wait for owner approval, then exchange the saved claim token for an app-resource or Sending-resource smx_agent_ token.
expectations: The output starts from /auth.md.
The output offers npx skills add Sendmux/skills when the platform supports Skills.
The output requests /agent-auth/agent/identity/challenge before /agent-auth/agent/identity.
The output says proof_of_work is base64 UTF-8 JSON containing the exact challenge and solution.
The output says registration includes proof_of_work at identity_endpoint.
The output saves the full agent credential bundle in a secure store before reporting success and exchanges identity_assertion at /agent-auth/oauth2/token.
The output keeps identity_assertion or the pre-claim smx_agent_ token available until the owner invite returns 202.
The output says claim_token cannot create the owner invite and is only for post-approval exchange.
The output does not poll the claim-token grant before the owner invite returns 202, then uses claim_token polling as the wait-for-approval path.
The output gives an example secure store such as 1Password, OS keychain, or the agent platform's encrypted secret store.
The output says not to store claim_token or agent credentials in chat, logs, repo files, screenshots, temporary prompts, or memory-only state.
The output says to stop and ask where to store the credential bundle if no secure store is available.
The output calls /api/v1/mailbox/me before the owner invite.
The output says pre-claim smx_agent_ cannot send.
The output invites the owner through /agent-auth/agent/identity/invite or asks: What owner email should I invite for approval?
The output says to register again and invite immediately if both identity_assertion and the pre-claim token were lost before invite 202.
The output waits for owner approval before requesting a Sending-resource smx_agent_ token.
The output stops and reports registration 503 server_error instead of retrying repeatedly.
The output uses Retry-After or retry_after for token-exchange 503 retries.
The output tells the agent to respect Retry-After or retry_after on 429.
